title: Pseudodissochaeta assamica
taxonomy: Pseudodissochaeta assamica is classified under the family Polypodiaceae. This plant is a species of fern and is native to the Himalayan region. It belongs to the genus Pseudodissochaeta, which is a group of epiphytic ferns. The classification of Pseudodissochaeta assamica is further supported by its unique characteristics and morphological features. As a result, it is often included in the list of rare and endangered species in the region.

intro: Pseudodissochaeta assamica is a plant species belonging to the family Cunoniaceae, native to the eastern Himalayas and Assam. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help in reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the body. The bioactive constituents of Pseudodissochaeta assamica include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
